Title: Engineering transkingdom signalling in plants to control gene expression in rhizosphere bacteria

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an amino group from L-glutamate to 3-O-methyl-scyllo-inosose to form 3-O-MSI, and also to scyllo-inosose to form scyllo-inosamine (SIA).
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Medicago truncatula; Hordeum vulgare; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: LjUBI1 for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; constitutive promoters in Medicago truncatula hairy roots; Oryza sativa ubiquitin1 promoter in barley
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in rhizobia during N2-fixing symbiosis in nodules as part of rhizopine biosynthesis locus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N2-fixing symbiosis in nodules; rhizosphere/root transformation context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transkingdom signal perceived by rhizosphere bacteria; can also serve as carbon and nitrogen source for rhizobia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root nodules for natural 3-O-MSI; engineered roots/rhizosphere exudates for SIA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables biosynthesis of 3-O-MSI in rhizobia and, with inositol dehydrogenase, enables SIA production in engineered plants, causing bacterial biosensor activation in the rhizosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis in nodules; heterologous expression; purified enzyme in vitro assay; linked enzyme assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; transgenic Medicago truncatula and barley metabolite profiling by GC-MS; rhizosphere biosensor assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Bioinformatic comparative locus analysis using IMG database; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for engineering rhizopine-mediated plant control of rhizosphere bacteria; should be stacked with idhA/iolG to generate SIA in cereals and legumes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mosB disruption abolished detectable rhizopine production in nodules. 2. Purified MosB converted 3-O-methyl-scyllo-inosose plus L-glutamate into 3-O-MSI in vitro. 3. MosB also accepted scyllo-inosose, enabling a synthetic route to SIA when paired with inositol dehydrogenase. 4. Co-expression with idhA in plants produced SIA that signalled to rhizosphere bacteria.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MosB has dual substrate flexibility, supporting both natural 3-O-MSI biosynthesis and synthetic SIA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes (+)-1D-ononitol at the axial C-2 hydroxyl group to form 3-O-methyl-scyllo-inosose.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: required step; key control point not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Rm1021 background for heterologous nodule expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in rhizopine loci and functions during symbiosis in nodules
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in nodules in heterologous S. meliloti Rm1021 strain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to generate the keto intermediate for 3-O-MSI biosynthesis; absence of mosE abolished detectable rhizopine synthesis in nodules.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; mosE mutant analysis; heterologous expression in nodules; GC-MS detection of intermediate/product
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Bioinformatic comparative locus analysis using IMG database; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Natural pathway not readily transferable to plants, likely due to membrane/electron transport requirements.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited utility for plant engineering because the complex likely requires bacterial membrane/electron transport context; motivated use of synthetic SIA pathway instead.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mosBCDEF were consistently associated with rhizopine-producing rhizobia. 2. mosE disruption eliminated detectable rhizopine synthesis in nodules. 3. Expression of mosDEF in non-producing Rm1021 generated a compound identified as 3-O-methyl-scyllo-inosose. 4. The complex is therefore assigned as an ononitol dehydrogenase producing the MosB substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to mosDEF collectively; mosE mutant used as the disruptive evidence. Transfer to plants failed when combined with McIMT and mosB.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes myo-inositol to scyllo-inosose, supplying substrate for MosB to produce SIA.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Medicago truncatula; Hordeum vulgare
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; constitutive promoters in Medicago truncatula; Zea mays ubiquitin1 promoter in barley
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in engineered leaves and roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration; root transformation; rhizosphere context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies scyllo-inosose precursor and, when co-expressed with mosB, enables SIA synthesis in plants and rhizosphere bacterial signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; co-expression with mosB; GC-MS metabolite profiling; transgenic Medicago truncatula and barley assays; rhizosphere lux biosensor; GFP biosensor on barley roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: idhA alone produced scyllo-inosose but did not activate rhizopine biosensor, indicating precursor accumulation without signal output.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core upstream enzyme for engineering SIA production in crops; useful in stacked constructs with mosB for controllable microbiome signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IdhA functions as a plant-expressed myo-inositol dehydrogenase. 2. Co-expression with MosB in Nicotiana benthamiana produced SIA. 3. Stable/co-transformed expression with MosB in Medicago truncatula and barley generated SIA in roots. 4. SIA exudation activated bacterial biosensors in the rhizosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected for plant engineering after the natural 3-O-MSI pathway proved difficult to transfer.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Rhizobium leguminosarum biosensor strain in rhizosphere assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: mocB promoter in lux/GFP biosensor construct with divergent mocR
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in biosensor bacteria carrying pOPS0046 or pOPS0761
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to SIA or (-)-1L-3-O-MSI in culture; rhizosphere exposure on Medicago and barley roots
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Rhizopine-responsive gene expression / catabolic locus induction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-responsive transcriptional activation of mocB promoter reporter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates transcription from mocB promoter in response to rhizopines
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: mocB promoter-driven reporter; rhizopine catabolism locus genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive sensor/regulator enabling bacterial perception of plant-produced rhizopine and conversion of metabolite presence into gene expression output.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Lux and GFP biosensor assays in culture and on roots/rhizosphere
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizosphere around nodules and engineered roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers rhizopine-inducible reporter expression in rhizosphere bacteria exposed to plant- or rhizobium-produced rhizopines.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential sensing module for deploying rhizopine-controlled bacterial functions such as N2 fixation, nutrient solubilisation, or biocontainment in the rhizosphere.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A biosensor using mocB promoter and mocR responded specifically to SIA and natural (-)-1L-3-O-MSI. 2. The biosensor detected rhizopine exudation from nodulated Medicago sativa roots. 3. The same sensing system detected SIA produced by engineered Medicago truncatula and barley roots, demonstrating synthetic transkingdom signalling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text mentions mocR1 in biosensor construction; functional focus is the rhizopine-responsive regulator driving mocB promoter induction.
